summary: Reference context is complete and well-structured. All affected areas (codegen, runtime/closure, runtime/gc) are covered by relevant specs. Spec plan correctly targets 3 existing specs for modification with appropriate sections. Minor cosmetic issue with placeholder names in the reference table does not affect spec plan validity.
findings: Minor: The reference context table shows '?' placeholders for spec paths instead of the actual spec names. This is a rendering issue in the artifact CLI — the spec_plan data below is correct and complete.
All affected areas are covered: codegen (jit-refcount.md), runtime/gc (gc.md), runtime/closure (closure.md), and supporting specs for value-and-rc, cranelift, and symbols.
Spec plan has 3 entries, all with valid main_spec_ref paths that include subfolders. All use 'modify' action with valid source paths pointing to existing specs.
Sections are appropriate: jit-refcount-enable gets overview+changes+test-plan (the primary spec), gc-reenable and closure-release-fix get overview+changes (supporting changes).
Each spec file covers exactly one logical unit. No duplicate section types needed.
No irrelevant specs included — all 6 referenced specs are directly relevant to the refcount enable work.
